title: Missing ara_artifact_paths in provenance record
severity: medium
location: src/agents/data_acquisition/agent.py:142-157 (_build_prov_record method)
problem: The _build_prov_record method constructs a PROV-DM compatible provenance record from AcquisitionResult, but omits the ara_artifact_paths field. AcquisitionResult includes ara_artifact_paths (line 36) which represents the paths to all ARA-generated artifacts (JSON/Parquet files in ara/artifacts/). This field is critical for provenance traceability — it documents which specific artifact files were produced by the acquisition and ingest pass, allowing downstream agents and human reviewers to trace analysis results back to the exact artifacts consumed. The missing field causes loss of traceability information in the prov_activities state record.
spec_or_invariant: AGENT.md §Outputs lines 48-49: 'ARA structured artifacts | ara/artifacts/ | JSON + Parquet | Controls, STRM mappings, maturity levels'. The evaluation criteria (line 91) expect 'ARA structured artifacts pass schema validation'. Provenance must document what was produced.
proposed_fix: Add ara_artifact_paths to the return dict: change line 156 from `"dvc_remote_confirmed": result.dvc_remote_confirmed,` to `"dvc_remote_confirmed": result.dvc_remote_confirmed,
            "ara_artifact_paths": result.ara_artifact_paths,` to include the artifact file paths in the provenance record.

21	from ..base import BasePipelineAgent

23	# ── Result type ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24	
25	
26	class AcquisitionResult:
27	    """Record of a completed data acquisition run."""
28	
29	    __slots__ = (
30	        "source_url",
31	        "github_commit_hash",
32	        "sha256_hash",
33	        "acquisition_timestamp_utc",

35	        "strm_mapping_count",

41	        self,
42	        source_url: str,
43	        github_commit_hash: str,
44	        sha256_hash: str,
45	        acquisition_timestamp_utc: str,
46	        control_count: int,
47	        strm_mapping_count: int,
48	        ara_artifact_paths: list[str],
49	        dvc_remote_confirmed: bool,
50	    ) -> None:
51	        self.source_url = source_url
52	        self.github_commit_hash = github_commit_hash
53	        self.sha256_hash = sha256_hash
54	        self.acquisition_timestamp_utc = acquisition_timestamp_utc

56	        self.strm_mapping_count = strm_mapping_count
57	        self.ara_artifact_paths = ara_artifact_paths

60

62	_MIN_CONTROL_COUNT = 1_200
63	_MIN_STRM_MAPPING_COUNT = 250_000
64

68	
69	class DataAcquisitionAgent(BasePipelineAgent):
70	    """
71	    Acquires SCF corpus from GitHub, verifies SHA-256, ingests via ARA Compiler.
72	
73	    Behavioral constraints (per AGENT.md):
74	      - Never modify raw XLSX after acquisition — write-protected from landing
75	      - Never acquire from non-authoritative source
76	      - Halt immediately if SHA-256 verification fails — do not pass downstream
77	      - All data stays local — DVC remote is local filesystem only
78	      - Never overwrite a prior acquisition without creating a new DVC version
79	      - License guard: SCF (CC BY-ND 4.0) pre-approved; all other framework
80	        full-text requires operator license sign-off in lab notebook before ingest
81	    """
82	
83	    AGENT_ID = "data-acquisition"
84	    PROTECTED = False
85	
86	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
87	        """
88	        Acquire SCF corpus, verify integrity, run ARA Compiler ingestion.
89	
90	        Phase 1 implementation will:
91	          1. Read configs/data_sources.yaml for GitHub URL, branch, expected sheets
92	          2. Clone/pull from SCF GitHub at configured commit (mcp-dvc)
93	          3. Verify SHA-256 against MANIFEST.md — halt via RuntimeError if mismatch
94	          4. Record github_commit_hash and acquisition_timestamp to MANIFEST.md
95	          5. Run ARA Compiler — convert XLSX to JSON+Parquet in ara/artifacts/
96	          6. Validate record counts: controls >= 1,200; STRM mappings >= 250,000
97	          7. DVC push to local remote (never SaaS)
98	          8. Append acquisition event to lab notebook (append-only, mcp-lab-notebook)
99	
100	        Returns partial HCGRCState update:
101	          prov_activities: acquisition provenance record
102	          phase_1_ready: remains False until DataStewardAgent writes splits

112	        Verify SHA-256 of acquired file against expected hash in MANIFEST.md.
113	
114	        Raises RuntimeError if mismatch — behavioral constraint:
115	        never proceed if SHA-256 verification fails.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError

120	        """
121	        Validate record counts meet minimum thresholds.
122

124	        Raises ValueError if below — triggers halt and human escalation.
125	        """

content.content:
1	---
2	name: data-acquisition
3	description: Acquires the complete SCF dataset from its authoritative source, verifies integrity via SHA-256 manifest, ingests it through the ARA Compiler into structured research artifacts, and hands versioned data to the Data Steward. The single point of contact between the platform and the SCF source — all other agents work from the versioned artifact, never the raw source.
4	version: 1.0.0
5	team: 02-data
6	status: primary
7	trigger: always
8	author: HC-GRC
9	tags: [Data Acquisition, SCF, ARA Compiler, DVC, Manifest, Data Integrity]
10	skills: [ara-compiler, ray-data]
11	tools: [mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook]
12	---
13	
14	# Data Acquisition Agent
15	
16	## Purpose
17	
18	Every empirical finding in this platform traces back to a specific version of the SCF dataset. If that lineage is broken — if the source is ambiguous, the acquisition date unrecorded, or the integrity unverified — the entire research chain is compromised. The Data Acquisition Agent establishes and maintains that lineage. It acquires from the authoritative GitHub source, records the exact commit hash and acquisition timestamp, verifies SHA-256 against the manifest, and produces versioned DVC artifacts that all downstream agents reference. It also runs the ARA Compiler ingestion pass, converting the raw XLSX into structured research artifacts.
19	
20	## Position in Workflow
21	
22	Runs once at project initialization. Re-runs when SCF releases a new version. All downstream agents block until acquisition completes and DVC push confirms.
23	
24	```
25	[SCF GitHub Repository]
26	        ↓
27	[Data Acquisition Agent] → SHA-256 verified raw data → DVC version
28	        ↓
29	  ARA Compiler ingestion → structured artifacts
30	        ↓
31	[Data Steward Agent]
32	```
33	
34	## Inputs
35	
36	| Input | Source | Format | Schema / Notes |
37	|-------|--------|--------|----------------|
38	| SCF acquisition config | configs/data_sources.yaml | YAML | GitHub repo URL, branch, expected sheets, STRM relationship types |
39	| Existing DVC manifest | data/01-raw/MANIFEST.md | Markdown | Prior acquisition records — used to detect if re-acquisition is needed |
40	| ARA Compiler config | ara/ directory | YAML | Ingestion schema for converting XLSX to structured artifacts |
41	
42	## Outputs / Artifacts
43	
44	| Artifact | Destination | Format | Schema / Notes |
45	|----------|-------------|--------|----------------|
46	| Raw SCF XLSX | data/01-raw/SCF/ (DVC tracked) | XLSX | Unmodified source file, never altered |
47	| DVC manifest entry | data/01-raw/MANIFEST.md | Markdown | SHA-256, source URL, GitHub commit hash, acquisition timestamp |
48	| ARA structured artifacts | ara/artifacts/ | JSON + Parquet | Controls, STRM mappings, maturity levels — schema per ARA Compiler spec |
49	| Acquisition log | Lab notebook | Markdown | Source, version, record counts, any acquisition anomalies |
50	
51	## Tools & MCP Servers
52	
53	| Tool | Purpose | MCP Server | Notes |
54	|------|---------|-----------|-------|
55	| DVC | Version raw data and push to local remote | mcp-dvc | Local remote only — data never leaves machine |
56	| Lab notebook | Record acquisition event with full provenance | mcp-lab-notebook | Append-only |
57	
58	## Skills Used
59	
60	- **ARA Compiler** — Converts raw SCF XLSX into structured, agent-consumable research artifacts. Handles sheet parsing, relationship type normalization, control ID standardization, and STRM mapping extraction. Produces the canonical data format all analysis agents work from.
61	- **Ray Data** — Parallel processing for MANIFEST verification and bulk artifact validation across 1,400+ controls and 280,000+ STRM mappings.
62	
63	## Handoffs
64	
65	**Receives from**: Orchestrator (acquisition trigger), configs/data_sources.yaml (source config)
66	**Passes to**: Data Steward (versioned artifacts for quality validation), Embedding Agent (control text for embedding)
67	**Human gate**: No gate on acquisition itself — but manifest hash and record counts are surfaced in the lab notebook for human review before research phases begin
68	
69	## Behavioral Constraints
70	
71	- Never modify the raw XLSX after acquisition — it is write-protected from the moment it lands in data/01-raw/.
72	- Never acquire from a non-authoritative source — only the SCF GitHub repository at the configured URL and branch.
73	- Never proceed if SHA-256 verification fails — halt and escalate to human.
74	- Data-sovereign: all acquired data stays local. DVC remote is local filesystem only.
75	- Never overwrite a prior acquisition without creating a new versioned DVC entry — all versions are retained.
76	- Never ingest commercially licensed regulatory framework full text (ISO 27001, PCI DSS full text, HITRUST CSF) without explicit operator license sign-off recorded in the lab notebook. These sources require a license agreement before ingestion. SCF (CC BY-ND 4.0) is pre-approved for this study; all others require separate approval.
77	
78	## Failure Modes & Recovery
79	
80	| Failure | Detection | Recovery |
81	|---------|-----------|----------|
82	| SHA-256 mismatch | Computed hash ≠ expected in MANIFEST.md | Halt immediately; do not pass corrupt data downstream; alert human |
83	| GitHub unavailable | HTTP error or timeout on clone | Retry 3× with backoff; if still failing, use most recent DVC version with human approval |
84	| ARA Compiler schema mismatch | Compiler returns validation errors | Log errors; do not produce partial artifacts; escalate to human — SCF may have changed structure |
85	| Record count anomaly | Controls < 1,200 or STRM mappings < 250,000 | Flag as potential incomplete acquisition; halt; human review required |
86	
87	## Evaluation Criteria
88	
89	- [ ] SHA-256 hash recorded in MANIFEST.md and verified against acquired file
90	- [ ] GitHub commit hash and acquisition timestamp recorded
91	- [ ] ARA structured artifacts pass schema validation — zero validation errors
92	- [ ] DVC push confirmed — local remote has versioned copy
93	- [ ] Record counts within expected range: ~1,400 controls, ~280,000 STRM mappings
94	
95	## Notes
96	
97	The CC BY-ND 4.0 license on SCF data means derived datasets cannot be published or redistributed. All DVC remotes are local. No SCF data, embeddings derived from SCF text, or analysis results leave the machine. This is both a data sovereignty requirement and a license compliance requirement — the License Compliance Agent (Team 16) monitors ongoing adherence.
98

2	DataStewardAgent — Quality validation, split enforcement, and test-set access lock.
3	
4	Validates acquired SCF artifacts against a pre-specified quality ruleset, executes
5	the train/validation/test split (seed from configs/seeds.yaml), and enforces access
6	control: exploratory agents receive train + val only; test split is unlocked only
7	after BOTH Gate 2 (SAP lock) AND Gate 3 (EDA review) are confirmed.
8	
9	Phase 1 stub: run() and get_split_paths() raise NotImplementedError.
10	
11	Required tools: mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook, mcp-sap-validator
12	Required skills: ray-data
13	See: agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19	from typing import Any, Literal
20	
21	from ...state import HCGRCState
22	from ..base import BasePipelineAgent, SAPViolationError
23	
24	# ── Result types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25	
26	
27	@dataclass
28	class QualityReport:
29	    """
30	    Output of the Great Expectations validation suite.
31	
32	    surfaced at Gate 2 for operator review. Critical failures block Gate 2.
33	    """
34	
35	    total_controls_validated: int = 0
36	    total_strm_mappings_validated: int = 0
37	    expectations_passed: int = 0
38	    expectations_failed: int = 0
39	    critical_failures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # referential integrity etc.
40	    anomalies: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # documented in codebook
41	    quality_report_path: str = ""  # data/03-processed/quality_report.html
42	
43	
44	@dataclass
45	class SplitRecord:
46	    """Metadata for a completed split operation. Immutable after Gate 2."""
47	
48	    seed: int = 0                  # from configs/seeds.yaml — changing post-Gate-2 is SAP violation
49	    seed_hash: str = ""            # SHA-256 of seed value — recorded at creation, checked at access
50	    train_count: int = 0
51	    val_count: int = 0
52	    test_count: int = 0
53	    train_path: str = ""           # data/splits/train/ (DVC tracked)
54	    val_path: str = ""             # data/splits/val/ (DVC tracked)
55	    test_path: str = ""            # data/splits/test/ (DVC tracked, access-locked)
56	    split_dvc_hash: str = ""       # DVC hash covering all three splits
57	    created_at_utc: str = ""
58	
59	
60	# ── Agent ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61	
62	
63	class DataStewardAgent(BasePipelineAgent):
64	    """
65	    Validates SCF artifacts, executes split, enforces test-set access lock.
66	
67	    The split seed in configs/seeds.yaml is the single most consequential
68	    configuration value in the platform. Changing it after Gate 2 is a
69	    direct SAP violation (equivalent to switching hypothesis tests after
70	    seeing the results). The Steward records the seed hash at split creation
71	    and compares it at every future access.
72	
73	    Behavioral constraints (per AGENT.md):
74	      - Never provide test-split access before BOTH Gate 2 AND Gate 3 are confirmed
75	      - Never silently resolve a quality failure — all anomalies in codebook
76	      - Never modify the split seed after Gate 2
77	      - Never re-run the split after Gate 2 without a full protocol amendment
78	      - Codebook updated before splits distributed to analysis agents
79	    """
80	
81	    AGENT_ID = "data-steward"
82	    PROTECTED = False
83	
84	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
85	        """
86	        Validate SCF artifacts and execute train/val/test split.
87	
88	        Phase 1 implementation will:
89	          1. Run Great Expectations suite against ara/artifacts/ — full validation
90	          2. Document all anomalies in data/codebook.md
91	          3. Block on any critical failure (referential integrity, > 1% broken refs)
92	          4. Load seed from configs/seeds.yaml; compute seed_hash (SHA-256)
93	          5. Execute stratified split at configured ratios (±2% tolerance)
94	          6. Write train/val/test to data/splits/ (DVC tracked)
95	          7. Access-lock test split via DVC pipeline stage with SAP gate check
96	          8. Update codebook with any split-related variable documentation
97	          9. Append split record to lab notebook
98	
99	        Returns partial HCGRCState update:
100	          data_split_manifest_hash: SHA-256 of MANIFEST.md after split
101	          data_split_seed: int seed value used
102	          data_split_verified: True after idempotency assertion passes
103	          phase_1_ready: True — signals data pipeline complete (after EmbeddingAgent)
104	          prov_activities: split provenance record
105	        """
106	        raise NotImplementedError(
107	            "DataStewardAgent.run() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
108	            "Requires: ARA artifacts from DataAcquisitionAgent, configs/seeds.yaml, "
109	            "mcp-sap-validator configured. See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md."
110	        )
111	
112	    def get_split_paths(
113	        self,
114	        state: HCGRCState,
115	        split: Literal["train", "val", "test"],
116	    ) -> list[str]:
117	        """
118	        Return file paths for the requested split.
119	
120	        Test split access requires BOTH gates confirmed — Gate 2 alone is NOT
121	        sufficient. The Data Steward AGENT.md is explicit: EDA review (Gate 3)
122	        must complete before the test split is unlocked. This is the mechanism
123	        that prevents confirmatory tests from inadvertently contaminating EDA.
124	
125	        Raises SAPViolationError if:
126	          - test split requested before Gate 2 (SAP lock)
127	          - test split requested before Gate 3 (EDA review)
128	
129	        Train and val splits are available after phase_1_ready = True.
130	        """
131	        if split == "test":
132	            gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
133	
134	            gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2", {})
135	            if gate_2.get("decision") != "approved":
136	                raise SAPViolationError(
137	                    "Test split access attempted before Gate 2 (SAP lock). "
138	                    "Gate 2 must be approved before test split is available. "
139	                    f"Current gate_2 decision: {gate_2.get('decision', 'not_run')}. "
140	                    "This is a SAP violation."
141	                )
142	
143	            gate_3 = gate_status.get("gate_3", {})
144	            if gate_3.get("decision") != "approved":
145	                raise SAPViolationError(
146	                    "Test split access attempted before Gate 3 (EDA review complete). "
147	                    "Both Gate 2 (SAP lock) AND Gate 3 (EDA review) must be approved "
148	                    "before the test split is unlocked. "
149	                    f"Current gate_3 decision: {gate_3.get('decision', 'not_run')}. "
150	                    "See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md — 'Never provide test-split "
151	                    "access to any agent before Gate 3 confirmation.'"
152	                )
153	
154	        raise NotImplementedError(
155	            "DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
156	            f"Requested split: {split!r}."
157	        )

160	        """
161	        Verify the split seed has not changed since Gate 2.
162	
163	        Changing the seed after Gate 2 is a SAP violation equivalent to selecting

172	                f"Expected: {recorded_seed_hash}, got: {current_seed_hash}"

1	"""
2	compute_data_split — deterministic, idempotent SCF corpus partitioning.
3	
4	Seed derivation: SHA-256 of the raw manifest file → int.from_bytes() → int seed.
5	Same manifest → identical seed → identical split on every independent run.
6	This is the idempotency guarantee tested in test_phase0/test_data_split.py.
7	
8	Split strategy: shuffle-and-slice on UNIQUE SCF control IDs (per SAP §7 / issue #60).
9	Semantically equivalent to GroupKFold when control_ids contains exactly one entry
10	per unique control. CALLER CONTRACT: control_ids must be deduplicated before calling —
11	one ID per control, not one row per STRM mapping. Passing mapping rows (280,000) instead
12	of control IDs (1,400) would produce leakage because set(train_ids) & set(test_ids) only
13	catches list-level duplicates, not the case where the same control's mapping rows land
14	in separate splits. The DataSplitAgent is responsible for deduplication before this call.
15	
16	No SCF control text appears in both train and test partitions (given the caller contract).
17	DataSplitAgent asserts len(set(train_ids) ∩ test_ids) == 0 before writing.
18	This assertion is logged and is a required Gate 1 artifact.
19	
20	Split ratios: 70% train / 15% validation / 15% test.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import hashlib
26	from pathlib import Path
27	from typing import Any
28	
29	import numpy as np
30	
31	# ── Seed derivation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	
33	
34	def derive_seed(manifest_path: Path | str) -> tuple[str, int]:
35	    """
36	    Derive a deterministic integer seed from the SHA-256 hash of a manifest file.
37	
38	    Returns:
39	        (manifest_hash_hex, seed_int)
40	    """
41	    path = Path(manifest_path)
42	    raw = path.read_bytes()
43	    h = hashlib.sha256(raw)
44	    manifest_hash = h.hexdigest()
45	    # 8 bytes (64-bit) → 1-in-2^64 collision probability between different manifests.
46	    # Using int.from_bytes on the raw digest (not the hex string) for portability.
47	    seed = int.from_bytes(h.digest()[:8], byteorder="big")
48	    return manifest_hash, seed
49	
50	
51	# ── Core split function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	
53	
54	def compute_data_split(
55	    control_ids: list[str],
56	    manifest_path: Path | str | None = None,
57	    seed: int | None = None,
58	    train_ratio: float = 0.70,
59	    val_ratio: float = 0.15,
60	    # test_ratio is 1 - train_ratio - val_ratio = 0.15
61	) -> dict[str, Any]:
62	    """
63	    Partition control_ids into train / validation / test splits.
64	
65	    Exactly one of manifest_path or seed must be provided.
66	    - manifest_path: derive seed from SHA-256 of the file (production path)
67	    - seed: use directly (test / synthetic data path)
68	
69	    Returns:
70	        {
71	            "train_ids": [...],
72	            "val_ids": [...],
73	            "test_ids": [...],
74	            "seed": int,
75	            "manifest_hash": str | None,
76	            "n_controls": int,
77	            "split_ratios": {"train": float, "val": float, "test": float},
78	        }
79	
80	    Raises:
81	        AssertionError if any control ID appears in more than one split.
82	    """
83	    if manifest_path is not None and seed is not None:
84	        raise ValueError("Provide manifest_path OR seed, not both.")
85	    if manifest_path is None and seed is None:
86	        raise ValueError("Provide either manifest_path or seed.")
87	
88	    manifest_hash: str | None = None
89	    if manifest_path is not None:
90	        manifest_hash, seed = derive_seed(manifest_path)
91	
92	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
93	    ids = np.array(control_ids)
94	    n = len(ids)
95	
96	    # Shuffle with seeded RNG
97	    shuffled_indices = rng.permutation(n)
98	    shuffled_ids = ids[shuffled_indices]
99	
100	    # Compute split boundaries
101	    train_end = int(n * train_ratio)
102	    val_end = train_end + int(n * val_ratio)
103	
104	    train_ids = shuffled_ids[:train_end].tolist()
105	    val_ids = shuffled_ids[train_end:val_end].tolist()
106	    test_ids = shuffled_ids[val_end:].tolist()
107	
108	    # ── Leakage assertion (required Gate 1 artifact) ─────────────────────────
109	    train_set = set(train_ids)
110	    val_set = set(val_ids)
111	    test_set = set(test_ids)
112	
113	    assert len(train_set & test_set) == 0, (
114	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(train_set & test_set)} control IDs appear in both train and test"
115	    )
116	    assert len(train_set & val_set) == 0, (
117	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(train_set & val_set)} control IDs appear in both train and val"
118	    )
119	    assert len(val_set & test_set) == 0, (
120	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(val_set & test_set)} control IDs appear in both val and test"
121	    )
122	    assert len(train_ids) + len(val_ids) + len(test_ids) == n, (
123	        "Split sizes do not sum to total control count"
124	    )
125	
126	    actual_train = len(train_ids) / n
127	    actual_val = len(val_ids) / n
128	    actual_test = len(test_ids) / n
129	
130	    return {
131	        "train_ids": train_ids,
132	        "val_ids": val_ids,
133	        "test_ids": test_ids,
134	        "seed": seed,
135	        "manifest_hash": manifest_hash,
136	        "n_controls": n,
137	        "split_ratios": {
138	            "train": round(actual_train, 4),
139	            "val": round(actual_val, 4),
140	            "test": round(actual_test, 4),
141	        },
142	    }
143	
144	
145	# ── LangGraph node wrapper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146	
147	
148	def data_split_node(state: dict[str, Any], manifest_path: Path | str | None = None,
149	                    synthetic_control_ids: list[str] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
150	    """
151	    LangGraph node: compute and record the data split in state.
152	
153	    In Phase 0: uses synthetic_control_ids with a fixed seed (no manifest).
154	    In Phase 1+: uses manifest_path to derive the seed from the real corpus.
155	    """
156	    # Phase 0 synthetic mode
157	    if synthetic_control_ids is not None:
158	        result = compute_data_split(synthetic_control_ids, seed=state.get("data_split_seed") or 42)
159	    elif manifest_path is not None:
160	        result = compute_data_split(list(state.get("control_ids", [])), manifest_path=manifest_path)
161	    else:
162	        raise ValueError("Provide manifest_path or synthetic_control_ids.")
163	
164	    return {
165	        "data_split_manifest_hash": result["manifest_hash"],
166	        "data_split_seed": result["seed"],
167	        "data_split_verified": True,
168	        "prov_activities": [
169	            {
170	                "activity": "data_split",
171	                "run_id": state.get("run_id"),
172	                "seed": result["seed"],
173	                "n_controls": result["n_controls"],
174	                "split_ratios": result["split_ratios"],
175	                "leakage_assertion": "passed",
176	            }
177	        ],
178	    }
179

42	## Outputs / Artifacts
43	
44	| Artifact | Destination | Format | Schema / Notes |
45	|----------|-------------|--------|----------------|
46	| Raw SCF XLSX | data/01-raw/SCF/ (DVC tracked) | XLSX | Unmodified source file, never altered |
47	| DVC manifest entry | data/01-raw/MANIFEST.md | Markdown | SHA-256, source URL, GitHub commit hash, acquisition timestamp |
48	| ARA structured artifacts | ara/artifacts/ | JSON + Parquet | Controls, STRM mappings, maturity levels — schema per ARA Compiler spec |
49	| Acquisition log | Lab notebook | Markdown | Source, version, record counts, any acquisition anomalies |
50	
51	## Tools & MCP Servers
52	
53	| Tool | Purpose | MCP Server | Notes |
54	|------|---------|-----------|-------|
55	| DVC | Version raw data and push to local remote | mcp-dvc | Local remote only — data never leaves machine |
56	| Lab notebook | Record acquisition event with full provenance | mcp-lab-notebook | Append-only |
57	
58	## Skills Used
59	
60	- **ARA Compiler** — Converts raw SCF XLSX into structured, agent-consumable research artifacts. Handles sheet parsing, relationship type normalization, control ID standardization, and STRM mapping extraction. Produces the canonical data format all analysis agents work from.
61	- **Ray Data** — Parallel processing for MANIFEST verification and bulk artifact validation across 1,400+ controls and 280,000+ STRM mappings.
62	
63	## Handoffs
64	
65	**Receives from**: Orchestrator (acquisition trigger), configs/data_sources.yaml (source config)
66	**Passes to**: Data Steward (versioned artifacts for quality validation), Embedding Agent (control text for embedding)
67	**Human gate**: No gate on acquisition itself — but manifest hash and record counts are surfaced in the lab notebook for human review before research phases begin
68	
69	## Behavioral Constraints
70	
71	- Never modify the raw XLSX after acquisition — it is write-protected from the moment it lands in data/01-raw/.
72	- Never acquire from a non-authoritative source — only the SCF GitHub repository at the configured URL and branch.
73	- Never proceed if SHA-256 verification fails — halt and escalate to human.
74	- Data-sovereign: all acquired data stays local. DVC remote is local filesystem only.
75	- Never overwrite a prior acquisition without creating a new versioned DVC entry — all versions are retained.
76	- Never ingest commercially licensed regulatory framework full text (ISO 27001, PCI DSS full text, HITRUST CSF) without explicit operator license sign-off recorded in the lab notebook. These sources require a license agreement before ingestion. SCF (CC BY-ND 4.0) is pre-approved for this study; all others require separate approval.
77	
78	## Failure Modes & Recovery
79	
80	| Failure | Detection | Recovery |
81	|---------|-----------|----------|
82	| SHA-256 mismatch | Computed hash ≠ expected in MANIFEST.md | Halt immediately; do not pass corrupt data downstream; alert human |
83	| GitHub unavailable | HTTP error or timeout on clone | Retry 3× with backoff; if still failing, use most recent DVC version with human approval |
84	| ARA Compiler schema mismatch | Compiler returns validation errors | Log errors; do not produce partial artifacts; escalate to human — SCF may have changed structure |
85	| Record count anomaly | Controls < 1,200 or STRM mappings < 250,000 | Flag as potential incomplete acquisition; halt; human review required |
86	
87	## Evaluation Criteria
88	
89	- [ ] SHA-256 hash recorded in MANIFEST.md and verified against acquired file
90	- [ ] GitHub commit hash and acquisition timestamp recorded
91	- [ ] ARA structured artifacts pass schema validation — zero validation errors
92	- [ ] DVC push confirmed — local remote has versioned copy
93	- [ ] Record counts within expected range: ~1,400 controls, ~280,000 STRM mappings
94	
95	## Notes
96	
97	The CC BY-ND 4.0 license on SCF data means derived datasets cannot be published or redistributed. All DVC remotes are local. No SCF data, embeddings derived from SCF text, or analysis results leave the machine. This is both a data sovereignty requirement and a license compliance requirement — the License Compliance Agent (Team 16) monitors ongoing adherence.
98

content.content:
1	# Agent Workflow Architecture
2	
3	**HC-GRC | LangGraph Topology Document**  
4	**Status:** Authoritative  
5	**Relates to:** ADR-0001 (LangGraph), ADR-0008 (Supervisor+Hybrid), ADR-0009 (Local Observability), ADR-0010 (Three-Tier Program), ADR-0011 (Resilience)  
6	**Required for:** NeurIPS 2026 reproducibility disclosure
7	
8	> **Program Context:** This document describes the agent workflow for Tier 1 of a three-tier research program (see ADR-0010). The workflow architecture and ARA artifact schemas defined here are shared across all future Tier 1 framework projects. They are designed as the data interface that Tier 2 (`hc-grc-comparative`) will consume. Any change to the ARA artifact schema or state key structure requires evaluating impact on Tier 2 ingestion.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 1. Overview
13	
14	HC-GRC is a 48-agent, 17-team autonomous research platform built on LangGraph 1.0 (GA: October 22, 2025). The platform executes a multi-phase empirical study of the Secure Controls Framework (SCF) across five analysis modules (P1–P5), enforcing an exploratory/confirmatory firewall at Gate 2 that is structural rather than procedural.
15	
16	This document describes the full LangGraph graph topology: agent nodes, edges, team subgraphs, interrupt() gate positions, state schema, fan-out/fan-in execution patterns, human-in-the-loop sequences, and the PostgresSaver persistence layer.
17	
18	**Agents do the work. This document describes what they will build.**
19	
20	---
21	
22	## 2. Architecture Pattern: Supervisor + Hybrid
23	
24	### 2.1 Decision
25	
26	HC-GRC uses a **supervisor-with-subgraph hybrid** pattern, not a pure swarm. See ADR-0008 for full rationale.
27	
28	At the macro level: a top-level `OrchestratorSupervisor` (Team 00) routes to 16 subordinate team subgraphs. Each team subgraph is a self-contained `StateGraph` compiled as a node in the parent graph. Analysis teams (03–04) use **fan-out/fan-in parallel execution** within their subgraphs. Gate teams (16) use **interrupt()** to enforce human approval before the workflow advances.
29	
30	### 2.2 Pattern Comparison (Why Not Pure Swarm)
31	
32	| Dimension                  | HC-GRC Requirement                          | Swarm | Supervisor+Hybrid |
33	|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------|-------|-------------------|
34	| Task interdependency       | High — Gate 2 firewall enforces ordering    | ❌    | ✅                |
35	| Intermediate validation    | Required at 5 gates                         | ❌    | ✅                |
36	| Exploratory/confirmatory   | Gate must be structurally enforced          | ❌    | ✅                |
37	| P1–P5 parallel execution   | P1–P5 modules are logically independent post-Gate 2 | partial | ✅ |
38	| Context window             | 1,400+ controls exceed single LLM context  | ❌    | ✅                |
39	| Error isolation            | Team failure must not cascade to other teams | ❌    | ✅                |
40	| Audit trail / provenance   | Every decision must be traceable            | ❌    | ✅                |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## 3. Team Topology
45	
46	### 3.1 Team Registry
47	
48	| Team | ID   | Name                         | Pattern           | Gate  |
49	|------|------|------------------------------|-------------------|-------|
50	| 00   | T-00 | Orchestration                | Top supervisor    | —     |
51	| 01   | T-01 | Research & Literature        | Sequential        | Gate 1|
52	| 02   | T-02 | Data Acquisition & Curation  | Sequential        | —     |
53	| 03   | T-03 | Analysis (P1–P5)             | Fan-out parallel  | —     |
54	| 04   | T-04 | Statistical Analysis         | Sequential        | Gate 3|
55	| 05   | T-05 | Hypothesis Management        | Sequential        | Gate 2|
56	| 06   | T-06 | Training & Fine-tuning       | Sequential        | —     |
57	| 07   | T-07 | Optimization                 | Sequential        | —     |
58	| 08   | T-08 | Infrastructure               | Sequential        | —     |
59	| 09   | T-09 | Evaluation                   | Fan-out parallel  | Gate 4|
60	| 10   | T-10 | Safety & Compliance          | Sequential        | —     |
61	| 11   | T-11 | Reporting & Communication    | Sequential        | —     |
62	| 12   | T-12 | Quality Assurance            | Sequential        | —     |
63	| 13   | T-13 | Provenance & Versioning      | Sequential        | —     |
64	| 14   | T-14 | Documentation                | Sequential        | —     |
65	| 15   | T-15 | Code Review                  | Sequential        | —     |
66	| 16   | T-16 | Gate Enforcement             | interrupt() nodes | All 5 |
67	
68	### 3.2 Macro Graph (Top-Level)
69	
70	```
71	START
72	  └─► T-00 OrchestratorSupervisor
73	        ├─► T-01 ResearchSubgraph ──────────────────── Gate 1 ──►
74	        │         └─► T-02 DataSubgraph ──────────────────────────►
75	        │                   └─► T-05 HypothesisSubgraph ──── Gate 2 (FIREWALL) ──►
76	        │                             └─► T-03 AnalysisSubgraph [fan-out P1-P5] ──►
77	        │                                       └─► T-04 StatisticsSubgraph ── Gate 3 ──►
78	        │                                                 └─► T-09 EvaluationSubgraph ── Gate 4 ──►
79	        │                                                           └─► T-11 ReportingSubgraph ── Gate 5 ──►
80	        │                                                                     └─► END
81	        └─► T-08/T-07/T-06/T-10/T-12/T-13/T-14/T-15 (support subgraphs, invoked on-demand)
82	```
83	
84	T-16 (Gate Enforcement) is not a sequential node in the flow — it is a cross-cutting concern. Its `interrupt()` nodes are embedded within the subgraphs where each gate fires.
85	
86	---
87	
88	## 4. State Schema
89	
90	### 4.1 Top-Level State (`HCGRCState`)
91	
92	All state flows through a single typed dictionary. Subgraphs share keys by convention; isolated state for internal subgraph use uses private keys prefixed with `_team_XX_`.
93	
94	```python
95	from typing import Annotated, TypedDict, Optional
96	from operator import add
97	from datetime import datetime
98	
99	class HCGRCState(TypedDict):
100	    # ── Execution control ──────────────────────────────────────────
101	    run_id: str                               # UUID, set at START, immutable
102	    phase: str                                # current phase label
103	    gate_status: dict[str, str]               # gate_id → "pending"|"approved"|"rejected"
104	
105	    # ── Research artifacts (append-only via reducer) ───────────────
106	    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # pre-registered hypotheses
107	    literature_refs: Annotated[list[dict], add]
108	    data_manifest: dict                       # DVC manifest pointer
109	
110	    # ── Analysis results (fan-out collectors) ──────────────────────
111	    p1_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # P1: STRM NLP
112	    p2_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # P2: Control Topology
113	    p3_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # P3: Regulatory Convergence
114	    p4_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # P4: Risk Blindspot
115	    p5_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # P5: AI Governance
116	    statistical_results: dict                  # post-Gate 3 confirmatory outputs
117	
118	    # ── Provenance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
119	    prov_trace: Annotated[list[dict], add]    # W3C PROV-DM events (append only)
120	    ara_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], add]  # paths to ARA artifact files
121	
122	    # ── Human gate payloads ────────────────────────────────────────
123	    gate_payloads: dict[str, dict]            # gate_id → payload shown to human
124	    gate_decisions: dict[str, str]            # gate_id → human decision
125	
126	    # ── Error / null results ───────────────────────────────────────
127	    null_results: Annotated[list[dict], add]
128	    errors: Annotated[list[dict], add]
129	```
130	
131	**Key design choices:**
132	
133	- `Annotated[list[..], add]` on every collector that parallel branches write to. Without this reducer, the last parallel branch's write silently overwrites all others.
134	- `gate_status` and `gate_decisions` are plain dicts (written sequentially by T-16 after each interrupt).
135	- `prov_trace` is append-only. No agent ever modifies an existing provenance record.
136	
137	---
138	
139	## 5. Human-in-the-Loop Gates
140	
141	### 5.1 Gate Architecture
142	
143	HC-GRC enforces five human approval gates using LangGraph's `interrupt()` / `Command(resume=...)` pattern. A persistent `PostgresSaver` checkpointer is mandatory — without it, frozen state cannot survive process restarts.
144	
145	**Gate positions:**
146	
147	| Gate | Phase                        | Trigger Condition                               | Blast Radius if Skipped        |
148	|------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
149	| 1    | Post-literature review       | Literature synthesis complete; SAP draft ready   | Wrong research questions locked in |
150	| 2    | Pre-confirmatory analysis    | **FIREWALL** — test split physically locked here | Exploratory contamination of confirmatory set |
151	| 3    | Post-statistical analysis    | All hypothesis tests complete                    | False inference advances to manuscript |
152	| 4    | Post-evaluation              | Final benchmark results available                | Flawed results cited in paper |
153	| 5    | Pre-publication              | Manuscript and ARA artifacts ready               | Non-reproducible research published |
154	
155	### 5.2 Gate 2 as Structural Firewall
156	
157	Gate 2 is not a soft checkpoint. It is the architectural boundary between the exploratory and confirmatory phases. The data split node in T-05 physically separates the test set **before** calling `interrupt()`. The confirmatory analysis agents (T-04 statistical tests) are not dispatched until `Command(resume="approve")` is received.
158	
159	```python
160	from langgraph.types import interrupt, Command
161	
162	def gate_2_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict:
163	    """
164	    CRITICAL: Do not move test split to after the interrupt.
165	    The split must happen BEFORE the pause so that the human
166	    cannot inadvertently influence what goes into the test set.
167	    The split is pure computation (idempotent), safe before interrupt().
168	    """
169	    # Pure computation — safe before interrupt(), will re-run on resume but idempotent
170	    split_manifest = compute_data_split(state["data_manifest"])
171	
172	    # Graph pauses here. Human reviews: hypotheses, SAP, proposed test split.
173	    decision = interrupt({
174	        "gate": "gate_2",
175	        "message": "Review pre-registration packet. Approve to lock test split and proceed to confirmatory analysis.",
176	        "hypotheses": state["hypotheses"],
177	        "data_split_manifest": split_manifest,
178	        "sap_document": "docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md",
179	        "warning": "Approving this gate is IRREVERSIBLE. The test split will be physically locked.",
180	    })
181	
182	    if decision != "approve":
183	        return {
184	            "gate_status": {**state["gate_status"], "gate_2": "rejected"},
185	            "gate_decisions": {**state["gate_decisions"], "gate_2": decision},
186	        }
187	
188	    return {
189	        "gate_status": {**state["gate_status"], "gate_2": "approved"},
190	        "gate_decisions": {**state["gate_decisions"], "gate_2": decision},
191	        "data_manifest": {**state["data_manifest"], "test_split": split_manifest, "locked": True},
192	    }
193	```
194	
195	### 5.3 Production HITL Requirements
196	
197	All five gates run against a persistent PostgresSaver. `MemorySaver` is prohibited outside unit tests.
198	
199	**Failure modes HC-GRC must mitigate:**
200	
201	1. **Abandoned gate (never resumed):** Background job scans for threads where `gate_status` is `"pending"` and `updated_at` is older than 72 hours. Sends reminder notification. After 7 days, marks thread `"expired"` and logs to null results.
202	
203	2. **Stale state on late resume:** After Gate 2 approval, the confirmatory analysis node re-validates that the data manifest hash matches what was displayed in the gate payload before proceeding.
204	
205	3. **Node re-entry side effects:** All code before `interrupt()` in any gate node is pure computation. Side effects (DVC lock, filesystem writes) occur after the interrupt returns.
206	
207	4. **Multiple sequential interrupts:** Gate nodes contain exactly one `interrupt()` call each. The UI layer must handle the queue gracefully — later gates are not rendered until earlier ones are approved.
208	
209	---
210	
211	## 6. Fan-Out / Fan-In: Analysis Subgraph (T-03)
212	
213	### 6.1 Pattern
214	
215	P1 through P5 are logically independent modules. They share the same input data (the locked SCF dataset) but do not require each other's outputs to proceed. This is the canonical use case for LangGraph fan-out.
216	
217	T-03 dispatches all five analysis modules in a single superstep. Wall-clock time for the analysis phase is bounded by the slowest module, not the sum of all five.
218	
219	### 6.2 Static Fan-Out (Fixed Modules)
220	
221	```python
222	from langgraph.types import Send
223	
224	def dispatch_analysis(state: HCGRCState) -> list[Send]:
225	    """Fan-out: dispatch all 5 analysis modules in parallel."""
226	    modules = [
227	        ("p1_strm_nlp",      {"module": "P1", **state}),
228	        ("p2_topology",      {"module": "P2", **state}),
229	        ("p3_convergence",   {"module": "P3", **state}),
230	        ("p4_blindspot",     {"module": "P4", **state}),
231	        ("p5_ai_governance", {"module": "P5", **state}),
232	    ]
233	    return [Send(node, module_state) for node, module_state in modules]
234	```
235	
236	Each analysis node writes to its dedicated results key (`p1_results`, `p2_results`, etc.) with `Annotated[list[dict], add]` reducers. The fan-in aggregation node fires after all five complete.
237	
238	### 6.3 Per-Module Fan-Out (Map-Reduce over Controls)
239	
240	Within P1, the 1,400+ SCF controls are processed in parallel using the `Send` API. The number of control batches is not known at build time — it depends on the SCF data loaded at runtime.
241	
242	```python
243	def dispatch_p1_controls(state: dict) -> list[Send]:
244	    """Map over control batches. Each batch → independent subgraph run."""
245	    batches = chunk_controls(state["controls"], batch_size=50)
246	    return [
247	        Send("analyze_control_batch", {"batch": batch, "p1_results": [], "module": "P1"})
248	        for batch in batches
249	    ]
250	```
251	
252	This map-reduce pattern is used in P1 (NLP similarity), P2 (graph topology), and P4 (risk blindspot). P3 and P5 operate at the framework level rather than the control level and use static fan-out over regulatory frameworks.
253	
254	### 6.4 State Isolation vs Shared State
255	
256	Team subgraphs use **isolated state** for internal processing (private to the team's subgraph) and **shared state** for outputs (surfaced to the parent graph via shared state keys).
257	
258	The boundary contract is explicit: a team subgraph's final node writes to the parent-visible keys (`p1_results`, `ara_artifacts`, `prov_trace`) and does not expose its internal intermediate state to the orchestrator.
259	
260	---
261	
262	## 7. PostgresSaver Checkpointing
263	
264	### 7.1 Configuration
265	
266	```python
267	from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
268	from psycopg import Connection
269	
270	def build_checkpointer() -> PostgresSaver:
271	    conn = Connection.connect(
272	        "postgresql://hcgrc_agent:***@localhost:5432/hcgrc_checkpoints",
273	        autocommit=True,
274	        row_factory=dict_row,
275	    )
276	    checkpointer = PostgresSaver(conn)
277	    checkpointer.setup()  # Creates required tables on first run only
278	    return checkpointer
279	```
280	
281	**Required on first run:** `checkpointer.setup()` creates the `checkpoints`, `checkpoint_blobs`, and `checkpoint_writes` tables. Idempotent on subsequent runs.
282	
283	### 7.2 Thread ID Strategy
284	
285	Each research run gets a unique thread ID. Gate sequences within a run share the same thread ID so the checkpointer can restore frozen state across gate pauses.
286	
287	```python
288	import uuid
289	
290	run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
291	config = {
292	    "configurable": {
293	        "thread_id": run_id,
294	        "run_metadata": {
295	            "project": "hc-grc",
296	            "version": "0.1.0",
297	            "initiated_by": "thomas@hcgrc",
298	        }
299	    }
300	}
301	```
302	
303	Sub-agent threads (for per-control map-reduce tasks) use `f"{run_id}::p1::{batch_idx}"` — scoped to the parent run, unique per batch.
304	
305	### 7.3 Time Travel and Debugging
306	
307	PostgresSaver supports state inspection at any past checkpoint:
308	
309	```python
310	# Get current state
311	snapshot = graph.get_state(config)
312	
313	# List all checkpoints for a run
314	history = list(graph.get_state_history(config))
315	
316	# Replay from a specific checkpoint (debugging, re-analysis)
317	past_config = history[3].config
318	graph.invoke(None, past_config)
319	```
320	
321	Gate 2 approval is an irreversible operational decision. Time travel to replay from before Gate 2 for debugging purposes is permitted in development but logged to INCIDENTS.md in any production replay.
322	
323	---
324	
325	## 8. Local Observability Stack
326	
327	LangSmith is disqualified (SaaS-only). The local stack:
328	
329	| Layer               | Tool              | Purpose                                    |
330	|---------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------|
331	| Tracing             | Arize Phoenix     | OTLP traces for all LangGraph nodes; local-first |
332	| Metrics             | OpenTelemetry SDK | Spans, latency, token counts               |
333	| Experiment tracking | MLflow (local)    | Run parameters, metrics, artifact lineage  |
334	| Provenance          | W3C PROV-DM       | Canonical vocabulary; Turtle serialization |
335	
336	**Phoenix configuration (local):**
337	
338	```python
339	import phoenix as px
340	from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
341	
342	# Start local Phoenix server (runs in-process or as a sidecar)
343	session = px.launch_app()
344	
345	# Instrument LangChain/LangGraph — captures all node traces automatically
346	LangChainInstrumentor().instrument()
347	
348	# All LangGraph invocations now emit OTLP traces to the local Phoenix server
349	# Phoenix UI available at http://localhost:6006
350	```
351	
352	No data leaves the machine. Phoenix stores traces locally. MLflow experiment tracking also runs locally (`mlflow server --host 127.0.0.1`).
353	
354	See ADR-0009 for the full observability stack decision.
355	
356	---
357	
358	## 9. Error Handling and Fault Tolerance
359	
360	### 9.1 Superstep Atomicity
361	
362	If any node in a parallel superstep raises an unhandled exception, the entire superstep fails atomically. Results from successfully completed branches in that superstep are preserved in the checkpoint but not committed to the parent state until all branches succeed.
363	
364	Implication: each analysis node (P1–P5) must catch its own exceptions and return a sentinel value (a null result entry) rather than propagating exceptions. This prevents one failing analysis module from canceling the other four.
365	
366	```python
367	def p1_strm_nlp(state: dict) -> dict:
368	    try:
369	        results = run_p1_analysis(state)
370	        return {"p1_results": results, "prov_trace": [build_prov_event("P1_COMPLETE")]}
371	    except Exception as e:
372	        return {
373	            "p1_results": [],
374	            "null_results": [{"module": "P1", "reason": str(e), "timestamp": now()}],
375	            "errors": [{"module": "P1", "exception": str(e)}],
376	        }
377	```
378	
379	### 9.2 Retry Strategy (v1.1 Middleware)
380	
381	LangGraph 1.1 (December 2025) introduced model retry middleware with configurable exponential backoff. This is the preferred retry mechanism for transient LLM API failures.
382	
383	```python
384	from langgraph.middleware import RetryMiddleware
385	
386	graph = builder.compile(
387	    checkpointer=checkpointer,
388	    middleware=[RetryMiddleware(max_retries=3, backoff_factor=2.0)],
389	)
390	```
391	
392	### 9.3 Abandoned Gate Recovery
393	
394	A background process (T-16 GateWatchdog) polls the checkpointer for threads in `pending` gate state older than 72 hours. It does not auto-approve or auto-reject — it sends a reminder. After 7 days without a response, it logs to `null_results/abandoned_runs/` and marks the thread `expired`.
395	
396	---
397	
398	## 10. Graph Compilation
399	
400	```python
401	from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
402	
403	def build_hcgrc_graph(checkpointer: PostgresSaver) -> CompiledGraph:
404	    builder = StateGraph(HCGRCState)
405	
406	    # Register all team subgraphs as nodes
407	    builder.add_node("t01_research",    build_research_subgraph())
408	    builder.add_node("t02_data",        build_data_subgraph())
409	    builder.add_node("t05_hypothesis",  build_hypothesis_subgraph())
410	    builder.add_node("t03_analysis",    build_analysis_subgraph())   # fan-out P1-P5
411	    builder.add_node("t04_statistics",  build_statistics_subgraph())
412	    builder.add_node("t09_evaluation",  build_evaluation_subgraph())
413	    builder.add_node("t11_reporting",   build_reporting_subgraph())
414	
415	    # Gate nodes (interrupt() wrappers, part of T-16)
416	    builder.add_node("gate_1", gate_1_node)
417	    builder.add_node("gate_2", gate_2_node)
418	    builder.add_node("gate_3", gate_3_node)
419	    builder.add_node("gate_4", gate_4_node)
420	    builder.add_node("gate_5", gate_5_node)
421	
422	    # Primary pipeline edges
423	    builder.add_edge(START,              "t01_research")
424	    builder.add_edge("t01_research",     "gate_1")
425	    builder.add_edge("gate_1",           "t02_data")
426	    builder.add_edge("t02_data",         "t05_hypothesis")
427	    builder.add_edge("t05_hypothesis",   "gate_2")      # FIREWALL
428	    builder.add_edge("gate_2",           "t03_analysis")
429	    builder.add_edge("t03_analysis",     "t04_statistics")
430	    builder.add_edge("t04_statistics",   "gate_3")
431	    builder.add_edge("gate_3",           "t09_evaluation")
432	    builder.add_edge("t09_evaluation",   "gate_4")
433	    builder.add_edge("gate_4",           "t11_reporting")
434	    builder.add_edge("t11_reporting",    "gate_5")
435	    builder.add_edge("gate_5",           END)
436	
437	    return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
438	```
439	
440	Support subgraphs (T-06 through T-15) are invoked by individual nodes within the primary pipeline where needed, not wired as top-level pipeline nodes. This keeps the primary execution path legible and the graph compilation deterministic.
441	
442	---
443

449	# Generates a Mermaid diagram of the compiled graph
450	print(graph.get_graph().draw_mermaid())

452	# For subgraph introspection

21	GateID = Literal["gate_1", "gate_2", "gate_3", "gate_4", "gate_5"]
22	
23	# Only these three decisions ever exist: the operator-response validator
24	# (_VALID_DECISIONS) rejects anything else, so the type reflects what can actually
25	# be written. ('pending' was never produced or routed on — removed pass-6.)
26	GateDecision = Literal["approved", "rejected", "deferred"]

29	# ── Reducers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	
32	def _merge_artifacts(a: list[str], b: list[str]) -> list[str]:
33	    """Append b onto a, dropping any value already present in a OR earlier in b.
34	    Order-preserving, O(n+m). The previous `x not in set(a)` deduped against a but
35	    not within b, so a single agent returning a duplicate path slipped through —
36	    violating the 'deduped on merge' contract (pass-6)."""
37	    seen = set(a)
38	    out = list(a)
39	    for x in b:
40	        if x not in seen:
41	            seen.add(x)
42	            out.append(x)
43	    return out
44	
45	
46	def _hypothesis_key(h: Any) -> Any:
47	    """The dedup key for a hypothesis dict. The canonical id field is 'hypothesis_id'
48	    (FormalHypothesis / SAP H[module].[n]); 'id' is accepted as a fallback. Keying on
49	    'id' alone silently never matched, defeating dedup (pass-7)."""
50	    if not isinstance(h, dict):
51	        return None
52	    return h.get("hypothesis_id") or h.get("id")
53	
54	
55	def _merge_hypotheses(a: list[dict[str, Any]], b: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
56	    """Append b onto a, deduping by hypothesis_id (latest wins, position kept).
57	
58	    Entries without an id (or non-dict entries) are kept as-is. Prevents a Gate-2
59	    reject → re-formalize loop from accumulating duplicate hypotheses into the
60	    pre-registered set."""
61	    merged = list(a)
62	    index = {k: i for i, h in enumerate(merged) if (k := _hypothesis_key(h)) is not None}
63	    for h in b:
64	        hid = _hypothesis_key(h)
65	        if hid is not None and hid in index:
66	            merged[index[hid]] = h
67	        else:
68	            if hid is not None:
69	                index[hid] = len(merged)
70	            merged.append(h)
71	    return merged
72	
73	
74	# ── Core state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

76

80	    gate_id: GateID
81	    decision: GateDecision
82	    rationale: str

91	      - MLflow  (tags.run_id)
92	      - Phoenix (trace attribute run_id)
93	      - PROV-DM (activity IRI contains run_id)
94	      - PostgresSaver (thread_id column)
95	
96	    See RUNBOOK.md for cross-store query procedures.
97	    """

100	    run_id: str  # UUID4, set at graph entry, propagated everywhere
101	    thread_id: str  # LangGraph thread ID (== run_id for single-run threads)
102	    phase: Phase

107	    # reducer protects against any future path that returns a partial update.
108	    # Fixes: ADR-0015 §75 serial guarantee (F1) + silent overwrite risk (F4).
109	    gate_status: Annotated[dict[GateID, GateStatus], lambda a, b: {**a, **b}]
110	
111	    # ── Failure events (written by gate nodes on rejection) ───────────────────
112	    # Agent Evolution monitors this store for routing re-optimization signals.
113	    failure_events: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
114	
115	    # ── Research artifacts (append-only) ──────────────────────────────────────
116	    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
117	    findings: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
118	
119	    # ── Data split record ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	    # Un-reduced (last-write-wins) scalars. Written by exactly one node per phase —
121	    # data_split_node in Phase 0, DataStewardAgent in Phase 1 — never concurrently,
122	    # so last-write-wins is safe by sequencing, not by a reducer (#212). Immutable
123	    # after Gate 1. If these ever become concurrently written, add a reducer.
124	    data_split_manifest_hash: str | None  # SHA-256 of the raw manifest file
125	    data_split_seed: int | None  # derived from manifest hash (int.from_bytes)
126	    data_split_verified: bool  # True after idempotency assertion passes
127	
128	    # ── Phase 1 execution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
129	    # phase_1_ready: True after data pipeline writes validated splits to disk.
130	    # exploratory_complete: True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub).
131	    phase_1_ready: bool

133

135	    # Written by EmbeddingAgent after embedding run completes. Required at Gate 2
136	    # to confirm which model produced the embeddings used in exploratory analysis.
137	    # Keys: model_name, model_hash, corpus_dvc_hash, timestamp_utc
138	    embedding_manifest: dict[str, Any] | None

146	    # eda_agent_statuses: per-agent completion records.

1	# ADR-0003: W3C PROV-DM as Canonical Provenance Vocabulary
2	
3	**Status:** Accepted
4	**Date:** 2026-06-09
5	**Deciders:** [REDACTED]
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Context
10	
11	HC-GRC requires verifiable lineage from raw SCF data through every transformation to every published finding. The platform needs a provenance vocabulary that: (a) is a published standard with long-term stability, (b) has a delegation primitive matching LangGraph's supervisor pattern, (c) is serializable to RDF for external queryability, and (d) is supported by Python tooling.
12	
13	## Decision
14	
15	W3C PROV-DM (https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-dm/) is the canonical provenance vocabulary for HC-GRC. PROV-O (OWL2/RDF) is the serialization format. Python implementation via `rdflib`. The seven core relations are mapped to LangGraph constructs in PROVENANCE_MODEL.md. Custom entity, activity, and agent types are prefixed `hcgrc:` per the namespace.
16	
17	## Alternatives Considered
18	
19	**OpenLineage:** Excellent for pipeline lineage, integrates with Airflow/dbt/Spark. Does not have a native delegation primitive (`ActedOnBehalfOf`) — it models job-to-job lineage, not agent-to-agent delegation. HC-GRC will emit OpenLineage events as a supplementary format (see ADR-0006) but PROV-DM is the primary vocabulary.
20	
21	**ML Metadata (MLMD):** Covers artifacts, executions, and contexts well. No agent or delegation primitive. Custom ContextTypes would be required for Gate, Hypothesis, and SAP concepts. More complex operational dependency (requires MLMD service). Eliminated in favor of PROV-DM + DVC.
22	
23	**Ad-hoc JSON lineage:** Tempting for simplicity. Rejected because it produces platform-specific provenance that requires a custom parser for external audit. PROV-DM gives the platform a standard that auditors, journal reviewers, and automated tools can consume without HC-GRC-specific documentation.
24	
25	## Consequences
26	
27	**Positive:**
28	- `ActedOnBehalfOf` directly models LangGraph supervisor → sub-agent delegation
29	- SPARQL queries over the provenance store answer audit questions in standard syntax
30	- W3C recommendation — stable, long lifecycle, extensive external tooling
31	- `rdflib` is mature and well-maintained in the Python ecosystem
32	
33	**Negative:**
34	- RDF/OWL adds conceptual overhead for contributors unfamiliar with the semantic web stack
35	- Fuseki or equivalent RDF store adds an optional but recommended operational dependency
36	- PROV-O serialization requires discipline to keep in sync with actual pipeline execution
37	
38	**Mitigation:** The Orchestrator emits PROV-O triples automatically at each node completion. Contributors do not hand-author provenance records — they implement nodes that emit them.
39

content.content: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/docs/architecture/PROVENANCE_MODEL.md

1	# Provenance Model
2	
3	**Standard:** W3C PROV-DM (https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-dm/)
4	**Serialization:** PROV-O (OWL2/RDF) emitted by the Orchestrator via `rdflib`
5	**Status:** Locked — vocabulary selection is an architectural decision (see ADR-0003)
6	
7	This document names W3C PROV-DM as the canonical provenance vocabulary for HC-GRC and maps its seven core relations to the platform's LangGraph topology. Without a named formal vocabulary, "provenance" becomes ad-hoc notebook prose — present everywhere, queryable nowhere. With PROV-DM, an external auditor can verify lineage from raw SCF data through every transformation to every finding, using standard tooling.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Why PROV-DM
12	
13	PROV-DM is the only W3C-recommended standard with a native delegation primitive (`ActedOnBehalfOf`) that models the LangGraph supervisor pattern directly. Its three core types — `Entity`, `Activity`, `Agent` — map cleanly to HC-GRC's artifact / pipeline-node / LLM-agent topology. Its `WasInformedBy` relation models agent-to-agent handoffs. No other fetched provenance standard has this combination.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## Core Type Mapping
18	
19	| PROV-DM Type | HC-GRC Instantiation | Examples |
20	|-------------|---------------------|---------|
21	| `Entity` | Any artifact produced or consumed by the pipeline | SCF XLSX, DVC artifact, model checkpoint, analysis result, finding, run manifest |
22	| `Activity` | Any LangGraph node execution (one step in one run) | `data-acquisition:run-001`, `p1-strm-nlp:run-001:exploratory` |
23	| `Agent` | Any LLM agent, human reviewer, or external system acting in the pipeline | `orchestrator-agent`, `thomas-jones` (human), `github-api` |
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Seven-Relation Mapping
28	
29	| PROV-DM Relation | Formal Meaning | HC-GRC Application |
30	|-----------------|---------------|-------------------|
31	| `WasGeneratedBy` | Entity was produced by an Activity | SCF curated dataset WasGeneratedBy data-curation run |
32	| `Used` | Activity consumed an Entity | p1-strm-nlp Used the curated dataset |
33	| `WasInformedBy` | Activity was triggered or informed by another Activity (Communication) | p2-control-topology WasInformedBy data-curation (handoff) |
34	| `WasDerivedFrom` | Entity was derived from another Entity | embedding artifact WasDerivedFrom curated dataset |
35	| `WasAttributedTo` | Entity is attributed to an Agent | finding H1.1 WasAttributedTo statistical-analyst-agent |
36	| `WasAssociatedWith` | Activity was associated with an Agent (responsibility) | p1-strm-nlp WasAssociatedWith p1-agent |
37	| `ActedOnBehalfOf` | Agent acted on behalf of another Agent (delegation) | p1-agent ActedOnBehalfOf orchestrator-agent |
38	
39	The `ActedOnBehalfOf` relation is the formal description of the LangGraph supervisor pattern: each analysis agent is delegated by the Orchestrator, which retains responsibility for the outcome.
40	
41	---
42	
43	## HC-GRC Entity Types
44	
45	These are `prov:type` annotations on Entity instances, allowing queries like "show me all SAP artifacts" or "show me all model checkpoints."
46	
47	| Type URI | Description |
48	|----------|-------------|
49	| `hcgrc:RawData` | Unmodified source data (SCF XLSX, literature) |
50	| `hcgrc:ProcessedData` | Transformed or curated dataset |
51	| `hcgrc:DataSplit` | Train/val/test split artifact |
52	| `hcgrc:ModelCheckpoint` | Fine-tuned or aligned model weights |
53	| `hcgrc:EmbeddingCollection` | Qdrant collection or FAISS index |
54	| `hcgrc:AnalysisResult` | Output of a P1–P5 analysis run |
55	| `hcgrc:Finding` | A specific result with SAP traceability |
56	| `hcgrc:SAP` | Statistical Analysis Plan (locked version) |
57	| `hcgrc:RunManifest` | Per-run execution record |
58	| `hcgrc:GateDecision` | Human approval gate decision record |
59	| `hcgrc:ProtocolDocument` | Any document in `protocol/` |
60	| `hcgrc:PublicationArtifact` | Paper, whitepaper, or dissemination output |
61	
62	---
63	
64	## HC-GRC Activity Types
65	
66	| Type URI | Description |
67	|----------|-------------|
68	| `hcgrc:DataAcquisition` | Downloading and verifying source data |
69	| `hcgrc:DataCuration` | Transforming raw data to processed form |
70	| `hcgrc:EmbeddingGeneration` | Producing vector embeddings |
71	| `hcgrc:AnalysisRun` | Executing a P1–P5 module |
72	| `hcgrc:ModelTraining` | Fine-tuning or post-training a model |
73	| `hcgrc:StatisticalTest` | Running a pre-registered confirmatory test |
74	| `hcgrc:GateReview` | Human review at an approval gate |
75	| `hcgrc:QualityAssessment` | QA Agent scoring a finding |
76	
77	---
78	
79	## HC-GRC Agent Types
80	
81	| Type URI | Description |
82	|----------|-------------|
83	| `hcgrc:LLMAgent` | An autonomous LLM-driven agent (any AGENT.md card) |
84	| `hcgrc:HumanReviewer` | A human acting at a gate or in review |
85	| `hcgrc:OrchestratorAgent` | The Orchestrator specifically (delegation root) |
86	| `hcgrc:ExternalSystem` | An external API or service (GitHub, timestamp authority) |
87	
88	---
89	
90	## PROV-O Serialization
91	
92	The Orchestrator emits PROV-O triples (Turtle syntax) at the close of each pipeline step. Example for a data curation run:
93	
94	```turtle
95	@prefix prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
96	@prefix hcgrc: <https://hc-grc.local/prov#> .
97	@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .
98	
99	# Entity: raw SCF data
100	hcgrc:entity/scf-raw-20260609
101	    a prov:Entity, hcgrc:RawData ;
102	    prov:value "sha256:abc123..." ;
103	    hcgrc:dvcRef "data/01-raw/scf/scf_2026.xlsx" .
104	
105	# Activity: curation run
106	hcgrc:activity/data-curation-run-001
107	    a prov:Activity, hcgrc:DataCuration ;
108	    prov:startedAtTime "2026-06-09T10:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
109	    prov:endedAtTime   "2026-06-09T10:45:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
110	    prov:used hcgrc:entity/scf-raw-20260609 .
111	
112	# Entity: curated dataset
113	hcgrc:entity/scf-curated-run-001
114	    a prov:Entity, hcgrc:ProcessedData ;
115	    prov:wasGeneratedBy hcgrc:activity/data-curation-run-001 ;
116	    prov:wasDerivedFrom hcgrc:entity/scf-raw-20260609 ;
117	    hcgrc:dvcRef "data/03-processed/scf_curated/v1" .
118	
119	# Agent: data-curation agent
120	hcgrc:agent/data-curation
121	    a prov:Agent, hcgrc:LLMAgent ;
122	    prov:actedOnBehalfOf hcgrc:agent/orchestrator .
123	
124	# Association
125	hcgrc:activity/data-curation-run-001
126	    prov:wasAssociatedWith hcgrc:agent/data-curation .
127	```
128	
129	---
130	
131	## Storage and Querying
132	
133	PROV-O triples are written to a local RDF store (default: Apache Jena Fuseki or `rdflib` in-memory export to `.ttl` files in `runs/<run_id>/provenance/`). Files are DVC-versioned alongside the run manifest.
134	
135	SPARQL query for "show me all activities that used the locked test split":
136	
137	```sparql
138	PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
139	PREFIX hcgrc: <https://hc-grc.local/prov#>
140	
141	SELECT ?activity ?agent ?time WHERE {
142	  ?activity prov:used hcgrc:entity/scf-test-split-v1 ;
143	            prov:wasAssociatedWith ?agent ;
144	            prov:startedAtTime ?time .
145	}
146	ORDER BY ?time
147	```
148	
149	This query answers the audit question: "Did any activity access the test split before Gate 2?" in O(n) time against the provenance store.
